name: Marlene Dietrich
description: German and American actress and singer (1901–1992)
abstract: Marie Magdalene "Marlene" Dietrich (, German: [maʁˈleːnə ˈdiːtʁɪç] ; 27 December 1901 – 6 May 1992) was a German and American actress and singer whose career spanned nearly seven decades. In 1920s Berlin, she performed on the stage and in silent films.

about_quotes.speaker: Alberta Hunter
about_quotes.evidence: She reminded me so much of Marlene Dietrich, you know, now that I know what it's all about.

about_quotes.speaker: John Altman
about_quotes.evidence: the Pasadena Roof Orchestra were hired to do the music for a film called “Just a Gigolo” which starred the totally unlikely combination of David Bowie and Marlene Dietrich.
about_quotes.doc_title: John Altman interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, September 14, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Jacques Butler
about_quotes.evidence: I don't know if it was Marlene Dietrich there or not, but I know they were singing that song at that time, in that cabaret.

about_quotes.speaker: Dave Brubeck
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Leroy Pearlman has mentioned that the Wolf Pack once played on a show with Marlene Dietrich.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave Brubeck — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
